sections.paragraph:
to measure (like the Syr., Chald., Arab. كال for كيل). In Kal it occurs once, Isa 40:12.

Pilpel כִּלְכֵּל-

(1) to take in, to hold, to contain; prop. used of a vessel (in fich halten, meffen). 1Ki 8:27, “behold heaven and the heaven of heavens cannot contain thee,” 2Ch 6:18.

(2) to hold up, to sustain.-(a) i.q. to bear, to endure (aushalten), Mal 3:2 Pro 18:14 Jer 20:9.-(b) to protect any one; Psa 112:5, to defend one’s cause before a tribunal, Psa 55:23.

(3) to nourish, to sustain, to provide with sustenance, Gen 45:11 47:12 50:21 1Ki 4:7 17:4 . Followed by two acc. Gen 47:12 1Ki 18:4, 13 1Ki 18:13.

Pass. כָּלְכַּל to be provided with food, 1Ki 20:27.

Hiphil הֵכִיל.-

(1) i.q. Pilpel. No. 1, 1Ki 7:26, 38 1Ki 7:38 Eze 23:32, מִרְבָּה לְהָכִיל “containing much.”

(2) i.q. Pilp. No. 2, a, Jerem. 6:11 10:10 Joe 2:11.
